events.label: reasoning
events.text:
The scenario describes a Lockout/Tagout (LOTO) situation where an energy source was missed - specifically stored hydraulic, gravity, or capacitor energy. This is a serious safety violation under OSHA 1910.147, which requires isolation and dissipation of all energy sources including stored energy.

Key points:
- Energy source missed (stored hydraulic/gravity/capacitor)
- Work is rough-complete stage
- OSHA 1910.147 applies
- This is non-compliant - stored energy must be isolated/dissipated before work proceeds

Decision: fail (non-compliant and dangerous)
Risk: critical (stored energy can cause serious injury or death)
Component conditions: non-compliant, installed-defective
Percent complete: rough-complete, maybe 70%
Confidence: high, 0.95

  "actions": [
    "Stop all work immediately and clear personnel from the danger zone",
    "Identify all stored/residual energy sources (hydraulic accumulators, gravity-loaded members, capacitor banks)",
    "Apply additional lockout/tagout devices and dissipate or restrain stored energy per OSHA 1910.147",
    "Verify zero energy state by test/try before resuming work",
    "Revise LOTO procedure to include stored-energy isolation steps and retrain affected personnel"
  ],
  "rationale": "OSHA 1910.147 requires that all energy sources — including stored/residual energy such as hydraulic pressure, gravity, and capacitor charge — be isolated, dissipated, or restrained before servicing. Missing a stored energy source means the LOTO is incomplete and workers are exposed to unexpected energization or release of hazardous energy. This is a critical non-compliance at the rough-complete stage.",
  "references": [
    "OSHA 29 CFR 1910.147(d)(4) — release of stored energy",
    "OSHA 29 CFR 1910.147(d)(5) — verification of isolation",
    "OSHA 29 CFR 1910.147(c) — energy control program requirements"
  ]
}
